findings.raw_text: Creatinine: 1.3 mg/dL (above baseline of 1.1 - mild acute on chronic)
Lactate: 2.4 mmol/L (ELEVATED - tissue hypoperfusion, concern for sepsis)
Procalcitonin: 18.4 ng/mL (markedly elevated - bacterial infection strongly suggested)
decreased urine output noted by wife
mild shortness of breath on exertion
Mental status significantly declined: GCS now 12, previously 14 on admission
New mottling bilateral lower extremities - peripheral hypoperfusion
Marginal improvement noted since morning. Patient now responding to commands about 60% of the time.
Still disoriented - does not know date or location. Oriented to person only.
Mottling on bilateral lower extremities slightly improved from morning

rag_guidelines_output.query_used: Symptoms: hypotension (moderate; BP nadir 94/60 mmHg), altered mental status (moderate; Mental status significantly declined: GCS now 12, previously 14 on admission), peripheral hypoperfusion (moderate; New mottling bilateral lower extremities - peripheral hypoperfusion), tachycardia (moderate; HR: 104 bpm), tachypnea (moderate; RR: 22 breaths/min), hypoxia (moderate; SpO2: 94% on 4L NC), fever (moderate; Temp: 38.7C), hypotension (moderate; BP: 98/66 mmHg), leukocytosis (severe; WBC: 18.7 K/uL), acute kidney injury (moderate; Creatinine: 1.6 mg/dL), thrombocytopenia (moderate; Platelets: 148 K/uL), sepsis (moderate; SEPSIS (pneumonia + E.coli urosepsis) - IMPROVING), acute kidney injury (moderate; AKI STAGE 1 (KDIGO) - WORSENING SLIGHTLY), septic encephalopathy (moderate; SEPTIC ENCEPHALOPATHY - IMPROVING), neurological response (moderate; Marginal improvement noted since morning. Patient now responding to commands about 60% of the time.), disorientation (moderate; Still disoriented - does not know date or location. Oriented to person only.), glasgow coma scale (moderate; GCS 13 (E3V4M6)), crackles (moderate; Crackles right lower zone persist), sputum (moderate; Sputum: thick, yellow-green tinged), urine output (severe; Urine output 0600-1400 (8 hours): 185 mL = 23 mL/hr), temperature (moderate; TEMPERATURE: 38.5C at 1400. Persistent fever), mottling (moderate; Mottling on bilateral lower extremities slightly improved from morning). Abnormal labs: Creatinine 8.9 elevated, Lactate 4.2 elevated, Platelets 95.0 decreased, Platelets 88.0 decreased, Platelets 92.0 decreased, Bilirubin 1.4 elevated, Hemoglobin 9.8 decreased, WBC 14.2 elevated. Retrieve the most relevant ICU clinical guideline evidence, thresholds, and diagnostic criteria for the flagged risks.

citations.rationale: SEPSIS_RISK matched: hypotension, altered mental status, oliguria, lactate > 2 mmol/L, WBC > 12 K/uL, creatinine elevated. Guideline from Sepsis-3 Consensus Definitions supports this risk. Evidence: SECTION 1 Sepsis-3 Definitions and Diagnostic Criteria Source: Singer M, et al. The Third International Consensus Definitions for Sepsis and Septic Shock (Sepsis-3). JAMA. 2016;315
citations.threshold: Sepsis-3: suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase >= 2

citations.rationale: SEPTIC_SHOCK_RISK matched: persistent hypotension, lactate > 2 mmol/L. Guideline from Septic Shock Identification and Management supports this risk. Evidence: Septic shock requires sepsis plus persistent hypotension needing vasopressors to maintain MAP >=65 mmHg and lactate >2 mmol/L despite adequate fluid resuscitation.
citations.threshold: Septic shock: vasopressor-dependent MAP >= 65 with lactate > 2 mmol/L after fluids

citations.rationale: AKI_RISK matched: oliguria, creatinine elevated. Guideline from KDIGO AKI Guidelines 2012 supports this risk. Evidence: KDIGO AKI definition: creatinine rise >=0.3 mg/dL in 48 hours, or >=1.5x baseline in 7 days, or urine output <0.5 mL/kg/hour for >=6 hours.
citations.threshold: KDIGO AKI: creatinine rise >= 0.3 mg/dL in 48h or urine output < 0.5 mL/kg/hr for >= 6h

citations.rationale: COAGULOPATHY_RISK matched: Platelets < 100 K/uL. Guideline from Sepsis-3 Consensus Definitions supports this risk. Evidence: Organ System Variable Measured Threshold Summary Respiratory PaO2/FiO2 ratio (mmHg) < 400 = score 1; < 100 = score 4 Coagulation Platelets (x10³/µL) < 150 = score 1; < 20 = score 4

EARLY_WARNING_ESCALATION matched: hypotension, altered mental status, tachycardia. Guideline from NEWS2 Early Warning Score supports this risk. Evidence: SECTION 9 National Early Warning Score 2 (NEWS2) Source: Royal College of Physicians. National Early Warning Score (NEWS) 2. Updated report of a working party. London: RCP, 2017. 9
citations.threshold: NEWS2 high risk: score >= 7 or any single severe parameter
